lesson.menu_title: Closing the Deal -- Confirming Terms & the Follow-up
lesson.menu_desc: Close the deal and write a short follow-up: confirm what has been agreed and what has been done with the present perfect (we have agreed on the terms; I have attached the contract; we have reached an agreement) -- have + past participle for results that matter now -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Closing the Deal: Confirming Terms & the Follow-up
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Roberto Rezende | Aula 18 -- Closing the Deal: Confirming Terms & the Follow-up

listenings.text: Hi Roberto, this is Amy. Great news on the engine deal. The client has just confirmed by phone, so now we need to close it properly and send a clean follow-up. Here is what matters. First, recap what you have agreed so there is no confusion later. The best tool for this is the present perfect, have plus the past participle, because it links a finished action to the result now. For example, you can write: we have agreed on a five percent discount, or we have reached an agreement on delivery. It tells the reader the decision is done and still true today. Second, list the next steps clearly. Say what you have done and what you will do. You have attached the draft contract, you have confirmed the delivery date, and you will send the invoice on Monday. Keep the tone warm and professional. Open with a thank you, confirm the terms, and close with a clear sign-off. Remember the difference between the present perfect and the past simple. Use have agreed when the result matters now, and use agreed yesterday only when you give a specific time. Keep it short, clear, and confident. Send me a copy before it goes out. Well done on closing this one.

This is a quick voice note about the engine deal. I have just closed it, and I am sending the follow-up email now. Let me recap what we have agreed. We have agreed on a five percent discount for the larger order, and we have reached an agreement on delivery in March. I have already attached the draft contract for their legal team, and I have confirmed the payment terms with finance. The client has signed off on the proposal, so the agreement is basically final. The next steps are simple. They will review the draft this week, and we will proceed to signature on Friday. I have also promised a free trial of the new model as part of the deal. I think the follow-up makes everything clear, because I have listed every point we have settled and every action I have taken. Once they confirm, we can finalize the contract. I will send Amy a copy of the email before it goes out, and I am confident this deal has been a real win for the team.

extra_audio.text: First, thank the client for their time and confirm the deal is done. Then, recap the terms you have agreed using the present perfect, we have agreed on the price. After that, list what you have done already, I have attached the contract. Next, state the clear next steps and who does what. Finally, end with a warm, professional sign-off.
